title: [APIv2]: podman hangs while using containers/$ctn/healthcheck
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
APIv2 endpoint containers/$ctn/healthcheck with curl will hang, as well as podman.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ❯ podman system service --time 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &
[1] 226187

2. ❯ podman run -d --name hc --health-cmd 'echo foo >&2 || exit 1' --health-interval=0 4fab981df737 sleep 60
2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4

3. ❯ podman healthcheck run hc
healthy

4. ❯ curl -v -X GET "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/hc/healthcheck"
```
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, GET is already inferred.
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/hc/healthcheck HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
[1]  + 226187 suspended (tty input)  podman system service --time 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
^C
```

Curl hangs there until `<ctr-c>`

**Describe the results you received:**
After stopping the curl process by `<ctr-c>`, podman hangs too.
❯ podman ps
^C
Check the background processes:
❯ ps aux | grep podman | grep -v grep
```
root      226003  0.0  0.0  80492  1904 ?        Ssl  04:45   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4 -u 2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4 -r /usr/bin/crun -b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4/userdata/pidfile -n hc --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket -s -l k8s-file:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4/userdata/ctr.log --log-level error --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4/userdata/oci-log --conmon-pidfile /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/lib/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /var/run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /var/run/libpod --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4
root      226187  0.0  0.1 2749668 35616 pts/0   TNl  04:45   0:00 podman system service --time 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
root      226309  0.0  0.1 2970884 34844 ?       Sl   04:46   0:00 /usr/bin/podman --root /var/lib/containers/storage --runroot /var/run/containers/storage --log-level error --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /var/run/libpod --runtime crun --storage-driver overlay --storage-opt overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on --events-backend journald container cleanup 2a3e1c61b12dda448d9a5b799c29402ed2611451cefcabee4c7fd648fa48eca4
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
In my understanding this API should work like `podman healthcheck run $ctn`, but the output is different as http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodRunHealthCheck says.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
❯ podman version
Version:      2.2.0-dev
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14.9
Git Commit:   a6f7afd41729edbe0d8cbf21bab5b475c6a13f9e
Built:        Wed Nov 18 01:56:43 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
